claim: Claude Code has three built-in sub-agents (explore, plan, general-purpose) that activate automatically based on task complexity without explicit user invocation
actionable: Understand when Claude automatically delegates to sub-agents: explore runs on Haiku for read-only file analysis, plan runs in plan mode for codebase research, general-purpose runs on Sonnet with full tool access for multi-file changes.
source_title: Every Claude Code Workflow Explained (& When to Use Each)

claim: Sequential flow pattern hits a context window ceiling where context rot prevents Claude from effectively finding information; use /clear and /compact commands to manage this
actionable: When running long sequential flows, periodically run /clear and /compact to preserve conversation summary without bloating context, or migrate to the operator pattern with separate terminals.
source_title: Every Claude Code Workflow Explained (& When to Use Each)

claim: The operator pattern (Claude -w with multiple isolated terminals) enables parallel task execution with clean context windows that don't interfere, automatic cleanup on close
actionable: Open multiple terminals with 'Claude -w [task name]' to create separate worktrees, each with full context window isolation; Claude auto-cleans up when closed.
source_title: Every Claude Code Workflow Explained (& When to Use Each)

claim: Split and merge pattern: Claude can fan out work to up to 10 sub-agents in parallel, but sub-agents cannot communicate directly—all coordination goes through the main agent hub-and-spoke
actionable: Use split-merge for independent subtasks (e.g., research 5 competitors in parallel); use builder-validator chain by having agents pass results through main agent to next sub-agent.
source_title: Every Claude Code Workflow Explained (& When to Use Each)

claim: Agent teams are marked experimental (Opus 4.6 research preview), enabled via CLAUDE_CODE_EXPERIMENTAL_AGENT_TEAMS=1 flag, use 4-7x more tokens than single session due to cross-collaboration overhead
actionable: Only use agent teams for genuinely complex multi-role tasks (e.g., frontend + backend + testing agents); set flag in settings.json and explicitly request team in prompt.
source_title: Every Claude Code Workflow Explained (& When to Use Each)

claim: Headless pattern (Claude -P 'prompt') runs without human interaction or approvals; best paired with cron scheduling for fully autonomous workflows
actionable: Use headless mode for batch processing with easy-to-verify outputs; guard with --allow-tools to restrict capabilities; implement REPL loop for iterative refinement until quality threshold.
source_title: Every Claude Code Workflow Explained (& When to Use Each)

claim: Community members using Ralph loop technique feed same prompt back iteratively so Claude refines its own work; some teams claim to ship entire projects overnight with this pattern
actionable: Implement iterative prompting: run headless agent, capture output, feed back with 'improve this output' prompt, loop until quality thresholds met.
source_title: Every Claude Code Workflow Explained (& When to Use Each)
